players.name: Alisson
players.bio: Brazil's undisputed No.1, Alisson is one of the finest goalkeepers of his generation. The Liverpool stopper has won the Champions League, Premier League, and FIFA Best Goalkeeper award. Cool as a caipirinha under pressure.
players.facts: Alisson scored a 95th-minute header for Liverpool vs West Brom in 2021 — a goalkeeper's dream, a striker's nightmare.

players.name: Éderson Silva
players.full_name: Éderson José dos Santos Lourenço da Silva
players.bio: A composed and dynamic central midfielder who caught the eye at Atalanta. Éderson Silva brings energy and intelligence to the engine room, combining defensive discipline with a sharp passing range. Still building his Seleção story.
players.facts: He shares his name with Brazil's other famous Éderson — the confusion at squad hotels must be spectacular.

players.name: Gabriel Magalhães
players.bio: The colossus at the heart of Arsenal's defence, Gabriel Magalhães is a ball-playing centre-back with a ferocious competitive streak. A Premier League title contender year after year, his rise from Lille to Gunners icon has been meteoric.
players.facts: Gabriel was born in São Paulo but grew up supporting Corinthians — something Arsenal fans politely overlook.

players.name: Marquinhos
players.bio: Brazil's defensive heartbeat and captain, Marquinhos has been a PSG fixture for over a decade. Elegant yet tenacious, he reads the game like a chess grandmaster. Now a senior statesman for the Seleção with 105 caps.
players.facts: Marquinhos joined PSG at just 19 for €31m — at the time, one of football's most expensive teenagers ever.

players.name: Casemiro
players.bio: Five-time Champions League winner and serial title-collector, Casemiro built his legend at Real Madrid as the ultimate midfield enforcer. Now at Manchester United, the veteran warrior brings experience and a tackle you'd hear from the car park.
players.facts: Casemiro won 5 Champions League titles — more than most clubs have managed in their entire history.

players.name: Alex Sandro
players.bio: A veteran left-back who has served Brazil with distinction across a decade, Alex Sandro has returned home to Flamengo after years at Juventus. Reliable, experienced, and still capable of marauding forward when the mood takes him.
players.facts: Alex Sandro spent seven seasons at Juventus, winning six consecutive Serie A titles — not a bad run at the office.

players.name: Vinícius Júnior
players.full_name: Vinícius José Paixão de Oliveira Júnior
players.bio: Arguably the most devastating winger on the planet, Vinícius Júnior terrorises defenders with pace, trickery, and an infectious celebration. The Real Madrid superstar is Brazil's golden ticket in 2026 — defenders, you've been warned.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Vini Jr. became the first Brazilian to win the Ballon d'Or in 2024, ending a 22-year wait for the nation.

players.name: Bruno Guimarães
players.bio: A tireless, tenacious midfielder who has become the beating heart of Newcastle United's renaissance, Bruno Guimarães combines relentless pressing with sublime technique. Widely regarded as one of the best midfielders in the Premier League.
players.facts: Bruno once played futsal competitively as a kid — and the quick-thinking it demands is still visible in his game today.

players.name: Matheus Cunha
players.bio: Electric, unpredictable, and impossible to contain, Matheus Cunha has been one of the Premier League's standout attackers at Manchester United. A forward who can play anywhere across the front line with equal menace.
players.facts: Cunha was born in João Pessoa but spent time in Switzerland's youth system before making it back to Brazil's senior set-up.

players.name: Neymar
players.bio: Brazil's record goalscorer and most decorated icon, Neymar returns to his spiritual home at Santos for what may be his World Cup swan song. At his peak, unmatchable. In 2026, the #10 shirt carries a nation's nostalgia and hope.
players.facts: Neymar's 79 international goals make him Brazil's all-time top scorer — ahead of even the great Pelé's 77.

players.name: Raphinha
players.bio: Raphinha has blossomed at Barcelona into one of Europe's most exciting attackers. The Porto Alegre-born winger is direct, creative, and has a knack for the spectacular. His rise from humble beginnings to Camp Nou is the stuff of Netflix scripts.
players.facts: Raphinha once played for Vitória de Guimarães on loan in Portugal — he's had more clubs than most golfers.

players.name: Weverton
players.bio: Veteran shot-stopper Weverton brings decades of top-level experience as Brazil's third goalkeeper. The Grêmio custodian was a Copa América winner in 2019 and a reliable presence between the sticks throughout his career.
players.facts: Weverton is the oldest player in Brazil's 2026 squad at 38 — old enough to remember when Ronaldo was Brazil's No.9.

players.name: Danilo Luiz
players.bio: One of Brazil's most versatile servants, Danilo has played full-back, centre-back, and midfield for the Seleção with equal composure. A Champions League winner with Real Madrid and now a Flamengo stalwart, he's seen it all.
players.facts: Danilo has won league titles in Portugal, Spain, Italy, and Brazil — the well-travelled overachiever of the squad.

players.name: Bremer
players.bio: A powerful, no-nonsense centre-back who made his mark at Torino before Juventus came calling, Bremer is one of Serie A's most respected defenders. Strong in the air, aggressive in the tackle, and growing in international confidence.
players.facts: Bremer hails from Itapitanga — a town so small it didn't even have a professional club when he was growing up.

players.name: Léo Pereira
players.bio: A commanding centre-back who has been a key figure for Flamengo's domestic dominance, Léo Pereira is physical, aggressive, and dominant in the air. Still establishing himself at international level but a well-respected figure in Brazilian football.
players.facts: Léo Pereira's physicality has earned him comparisons to a brick wall — his opponents would largely agree.

players.name: Douglas Santos
players.full_name: Douglas dos Santos Justino de Melo
players.bio: Left-back Douglas Santos has carved out a solid career across Europe including spells at Hamburg and Zenit Saint Petersburg. A dependable, attacking full-back who provides solid cover and experience in Brazil's defensive options.
players.facts: Douglas Santos plays in Russia's Premier League for Zenit — making him one of the squad's more exotic commuters.

players.name: Fabinho
players.bio: Once the lynchpin of Liverpool's Premier League and Champions League-winning side, Fabinho is a midfield enforcer of the highest order. Now in Saudi Arabia with Al-Ittihad, the defensive midfielder brings peerless experience to the squad.
players.facts: Fabinho started his career as a right-back at Monaco before being reinvented as a defensive midfielder — best position switch ever?

players.name: Danilo Santos
players.bio: A young, energetic central midfielder who has impressed for Botafogo, Danilo Santos has already found the net twice in just four international appearances — not bad for a 25-year-old still making his name on the global stage.
players.facts: With 2 goals in 4 caps, Danilo Santos has a better international scoring rate than Casemiro. Don't tell Casemiro.

players.name: Endrick
players.bio: The teenager who made the world stop and stare. Endrick announced himself on the global stage with audacious goals for Brazil before turning 18. Now at Lyon, this wonderkid is Brazil's most thrilling young talent since... well, Vinícius.
players.facts: Endrick scored on his Brazil debut aged 16 — becoming one of the youngest ever to score for the Seleção.

players.name: Lucas Paquetá
players.bio: The man who shares a name with an island paradise off Rio de Janeiro, Lucas Paquetá brings flair, vision, and creativity to Brazil's midfield. Now back at Flamengo after his West Ham chapter, he remains one of the Seleção's most elegant operators.
players.facts: Paquetá was actually born on the island of Paquetá in Rio de Janeiro's Guanabara Bay — his name was destiny.

players.name: Luiz Henrique
players.full_name: Luiz Henrique André Rosa da Silva
players.bio: A pacey, direct right winger with an eye for goal, Luiz Henrique has made a name for himself in Russia with Zenit after earlier stints in Brazil and Spain. Quick feet, quick thinking, and growing international pedigree.
players.facts: Luiz Henrique played for Betis in La Liga before heading to Russia — the opposite of the usual football career trajectory.

players.name: Gabriel Martinelli
players.bio: Gabriel Martinelli's relentless pressing, pace, and goalscoring instinct have made him a fan favourite at Arsenal. Born in Guarulhos, the 24-year-old left winger has thrived under Mikel Arteta and is ready to shine on the biggest stage.
players.facts: Martinelli was signed by Arsenal from Ituano — a small Brazilian club — for just €6m. Bargain of the century.

players.name: Ederson Moraes
players.bio: Once Manchester City's eccentric, sweeper-keeper extraordinaire, Ederson Moraes is now at Fenerbahçe but remains one of the most technically gifted goalkeepers Brazil has produced. His passing would embarrass most midfielders.
players.facts: Ederson once registered an assist from a goal kick — proving goalkeepers are, in fact, technically outfield players now.

players.name: Roger Ibañez
players.bio: A tenacious and aggressive centre-back who made his name at Roma under José Mourinho, Roger Ibañez now plies his trade in Saudi Arabia with Al-Ahli. Still only 27, he's a physical presence with the temperament of someone who takes no prisoners.
players.facts: Despite the very Spanish-sounding surname, Roger Ibañez is thoroughly Brazilian — born in the wine town of Canela, RS.

players.name: Igor Thiago
players.bio: A powerful, direct centre-forward who joined Brentford from Club Brugge, Igor Thiago has Premier League physicality and a sharp instinct in front of goal. With 2 goals in 4 caps, he's making a compelling case for a starting role.
players.facts: Igor Thiago grew up in Gama, a satellite city of Brazil's capital Brasília — home city of both him and Endrick.

players.name: Rayan
players.bio: The most exotic birthplace in the squad — Montreuil, France — Rayan is a Brazilian left-back who came through French football before earning his Seleção call-up. Just 19, he's already scored internationally and looks a thrilling prospect.
players.facts: Rayan was born in France but chose to represent Brazil — and has already scored for them. Vive le Brasil!
